$comment: c81 (the sqlite shell.c CLI route): ship <sys/resource.h> — getrusage + RUSAGE_SELF(=0, `constants`: shell.c's uses are unguarded calls, no #ifdef) + the glibc-exact struct rusage (2 struct timeval + 14 i64, sizeof 144 — libc FILLS the caller's object, layout verified against bits/types/struct_rusage.h, x86_64 == arm64). Run-proof on real libc: rc==0, nested .ru_utime.tv_sec/tv_usec sane, the last field (ru_nivcsw @136) overwritten from -1 to >=0, frame sentinel intact. Darwin's timeval diverges (tv_usec i32), which the per-format `variants` in BOTH sys/time.json and sys/resource.json now model, so the macho leg is carried too (see `targets`). ★ D-LANG-TYPE-IDENTITY-VOCABULARY (2026-07-20): main.c now includes <sys/time.h> BEFORE <sys/resource.h> — the order that used to fail S000D when the two descriptors spelled the `timeval` tag differently — and cross-assigns `struct timeval tv = r.ru_utime`, which only type-checks when both declarations intern to ONE TypeId. RED-ON-DISABLE: remove sys/resource.json -> F001A; drop either descriptor's `i64 "long"` timeval tags -> F_ShippedTypeIdentityConflict at compile.
